# Window Management Quirks

Platform differences in borderless windows, topmost, focus, modality, and Wayland.

## `overrideredirect(True)`

| Behavior | Windows | Linux/X11 |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| Removes window frame | Yes [35] | Yes [36] |
| Removes taskbar entry | Yes (WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW) [35] | Varies by WM [37] |
| Focus behavior | Works normally [36] | Broken — window may not receive focus [36] |
| Dialog stacking | Dialogs appear in front [36] | Dialogs may appear behind [36] |

### Windows Taskbar Workaround

On Windows, use ctypes to force a taskbar button for overrideredirect windows [35]:

```python
import ctypes
GWL_EXSTYLE = -20
WS_EX_APPWINDOW = 0x40000
WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW = 0x80

hwnd = ctypes.windll.user32.GetParent(win.winfo_id())
style = ctypes.windll.user32.GetWindowLongW(hwnd, GWL_EXSTYLE)
style = (style | WS_EX_APPWINDOW) & ~WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW
ctypes.windll.user32.SetWindowLongW(hwnd, GWL_EXSTYLE, style)
```

This is Windows-specific and not portable [35].

## `-topmost` Attribute

`wm_attributes("-topmost", True)` works reliably on Windows and macOS but some
Linux window managers may ignore it [38].

### Flash-Topmost Pattern

Raise window without permanent always-on-top [39]:

```python
root.attributes('-topmost', True)
root.attributes('-topmost', False)  # Immediately remove
```

### Combining with `focus_force()`

For best reliability across platforms [39]:

```python
root.attributes('-topmost', True)
root.focus_force()
root.attributes('-topmost', False)
```

## Focus Stealing Prevention

Linux window managers (GNOME, KDE, Cinnamon) actively enforce focus-stealing
prevention policies [40][41]:

- `focus_force()` and `lift()` may be blocked or deferred [40]
- On X11, `XMapRaised` pushes the window to the top of the stack — no standard
  way to avoid this [42]
- No universal workaround exists [42]

## Modal Dialogs (`transient` + `grab_set`)

| Behavior | Windows | Linux/X11 |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| `grab_set()` blocks parent | Reliable | WM may ignore [43][44] |
| Parent can be closed | No | Yes, on some WMs [43] |
| `transient()` hides from taskbar | Yes [46] | Yes |
| `transient()` iconify with parent | Yes (with bugs [46]) | Yes |

### Proper Modal Sequence

Call `wait_visibility()` before `grab_set()` to avoid grab errors [45]:

```python
dialog = tk.Toplevel(parent)
dialog.transient(parent)
dialog.wait_visibility()      # Ensure window appeared
dialog.grab_set()             # Input grab
dialog.protocol("WM_DELETE_WINDOW", on_close)
parent.wait_window(dialog)    # Block until dialog closes
```

### Known Bug

CPython #26340: Parent window with transient child may fail to iconify properly [46].

## Wayland

**Tkinter has no native Wayland support** [47][48]. It runs via XWayland only.

| Feature | XWayland Status |
|---------|----------------|
| Window position (`geometry()`) | Broken — affects size but not position [49] |
| Rendering | Race conditions cause corruption [50] |
| System tray (pystray xorg) | Does not work under Wayland [56] |

Feature request opened December 2024 (CPython #128204) [48].

## Multi-Monitor

| API | Windows | Linux/Mac |
|-----|---------|-----------|
| `winfo_screenwidth()` | Primary monitor only [51] | Combined across all monitors [51] |
| `winfo_x()` on secondary | Large positive or negative values [51] | Same [51] |

The `screeninfo` library provides reliable per-monitor detection across
platforms [52].

## Gaps and Limitations

- `-topmost` behavior on tiling WMs (i3, Sway) is undocumented for tkinter
- `grab_set()` reliability cannot be guaranteed on Linux
- Wayland positioning is fundamentally broken; no Tk-level fix expected until
  native Wayland support is implemented
